name: Paul Barbarin
abstract: Adolphe Paul Barbarin (May 5, 1899 – February 17, 1969) was an American jazz drummer from New Orleans.

works_created.t: Why Don't You Go Down to New Orleans

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: Luis Russell was the bandleader, and in the band was Paul Barbarin, Red Allen, Charlie Holmes, and Luis Russell playing piano

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: I joined Russell's orchestra... Paul Barbarin was the drummer.

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Did you listen to any other New Orleans drummers, like either Paul Barbarin or Zutty Singleton? [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah. Dannie Richmond, Paul Barbarin, and somebody else down there – Earl Palmer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Albany Leon "Barney" Bigard
about_quotes.evidence: Paul Barbarin and I -- he used to stay at my house... we'd sit in the back there and we'd dig up little ideas and I'd write them down and we sent to Washington to the copyright people and copyright them

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: Paul Barbarin is my godfather. That's my mother's oldest brother... Paul Barbarin was the oldest brother who was a drummer... And from that, you can see the four boys beatin' around the house on drums

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer lists Paul Barbarin as someone the interviewer has worked with, not the interviewee. The interviewee does not mention a personal connection to Paul Barbarin.
